---
title: "HERA_08a_Explore_Single_GAMs"
author: "Tory Eisenlohr-Moul"
date: "2026-03-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(scales)
```

```{r load-data}
gam_df <- read.csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/00 - CLEAR Lab (Locked Folders)/02 - Data Management, Analysis, and Papers/Studies_Projects/HERA/03_analytic_projects/HERA-Primary ms/03_dataedits_output/06_Single_GAMs/GAM_Reports_20260319/participant_significance_summary_20260319.csv")
```

```{r}
names(gam_df)
unique(gam_df$significant)
```


```{r}

# Calculate Significance
gam_df <- gam_df %>%
  mutate(significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant"))

# make it again but sort the bars by the number of significant participants
df_summary <- gam_df %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarise(significant_count = sum(significant == "Significant")) %>%
  arrange(desc(significant_count))

ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = reorder(variable, -significant_count), y = significant_count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Number of Participants with Significant Effects by Outcome", x = "Outcome",
       y = "Count of Significant Participants") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```


```{r}
# plot the distribution of range among ids with and without significance 

ggplot(gam_df, aes(x = significant, y = range)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of DRSP_NA Range by Significance", x = "Significance", y = "Range") +
  theme_minimal()

# now with histograms

ggplot(gam_df, aes(x = range, fill = significant)) +
  geom_histogram(position = "dodge", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of DRSP_NA Range by Significance", x = "Range", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "salmon")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())
```
```{r}

# =========================================================
# PERSON-SPECIFIC GAM CYCLICITY SUMMARY AND THRESHOLD PLOTS
# =========================================================
# Expected variables in data:
# "id" "variable" "outcome_label" "p.value" "edf" "n"
# "range" "N" "significant"
#
# What this code does:
# 1. Cleans significance variable
# 2. Filters to one DRSP outcome (e.g., "drsp_NA")
# 3. Summarizes range by significance
# 4. Fits logistic models predicting GAM significance from range
#    (optionally controlling for n)
# 5. Plots probability of significance across range
# 6. Computes summary stats at candidate thresholds
# 7. Lets you run the same workflow across many DRSP variables
#
# Packages: loaded in setup chunk above (tidyverse, broom, scales)
# Data: loaded in load-data chunk above as gam_df

# =========================================================
# 2. BASIC CLEANING
# =========================================================
clean_gam_summary <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      significant_num = ifelse(significant == "Significant", 1, 0),
      significant_f = factor(significant_num, 
                             levels = c(0, 1), 
                             labels = c("Not Significant", "Significant")),
      range = as.numeric(range),
      n = as.numeric(n),
      variable = as.character(variable),
      id = as.character(id)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(range), !is.na(significant_num), !is.na(variable), !is.na(id))
}

# =========================================================
# 3. FILTER TO ONE OUTCOME
# =========================================================
# Change target_var to whatever DRSP variable you want
target_var <- "drsp_NA"

get_outcome_df <- function(df, target_var) {
  df %>%
    filter(variable == target_var) %>%
    distinct(id, variable, .keep_all = TRUE)
}

# =========================================================
# 4. DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY FUNCTION
# =========================================================
summarize_cyclicity <- function(df_outcome) {
  overall <- df_outcome %>%
    summarise(
      n_participants = n(),
      n_significant = sum(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  by_sig <- df_outcome %>%
    group_by(significant_f) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_range = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_range = sd(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_range = median(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25_range = quantile(range, .25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75_range = quantile(range, .75, na.rm = TRUE),
      min_range = min(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_range = max(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_n = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  list(overall = overall, by_significance = by_sig)
}

# =========================================================
# 5. THRESHOLD TABLE
# =========================================================
threshold_table <- function(df_outcome,
                            thresholds = c(0.10, 0.25, 0.36, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)) {
  map_dfr(thresholds, function(th) {
    tmp <- df_outcome %>% filter(range >= th)
    
    tibble(
      threshold = th,
      n_at_or_above = nrow(tmp),
      prop_of_sample = ifelse(nrow(df_outcome) > 0, nrow(tmp) / nrow(df_outcome), NA_real_),
      n_significant = sum(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = ifelse(nrow(tmp) > 0, mean(tmp$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_)
    )
  })
}

# =========================================================
# 6. LOGISTIC MODELS
# =========================================================
fit_logistic_models <- function(df_outcome) {
  model_range <- glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  model_range_n <- glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_outcome)
  
  list(
    model_range = model_range,
    model_range_n = model_range_n,
    tidy_range = broom::tidy(model_range, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE),
    tidy_range_n = broom::tidy(model_range_n, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 7. PREDICTION DATA FOR PLOTTING
# =========================================================
make_prediction_df <- function(df_outcome, model_range, model_range_n = NULL, n_value = NULL) {
  xseq <- seq(min(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              max(df_outcome$range, na.rm = TRUE),
              length.out = 300)
  
  pred1 <- tibble(range = xseq) %>%
    mutate(
      pred_prob = predict(model_range, newdata = ., type = "response"),
      model = "Range only"
    )
  
  if (!is.null(model_range_n)) {
    if (is.null(n_value)) n_value <- median(df_outcome$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    pred2 <- tibble(range = xseq, n = n_value) %>%
      mutate(
        pred_prob = predict(model_range_n, newdata = ., type = "response"),
        model = paste0("Range + n (n fixed at ", round(n_value, 1), ")")
      ) %>%
      select(range, pred_prob, model)
    
    bind_rows(pred1, pred2)
  } else {
    pred1
  }
}

# =========================================================
# 8. PLOT: HISTOGRAM + LOGISTIC CURVE
# =========================================================
plot_range_histogram <- function(df_outcome) {
  ggplot(df_outcome, aes(x = range, fill = significant_f)) +
    geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.65, bins = 30) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Histogram of ", unique(df_outcome$variable), " Range by Significance"),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Count",
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

plot_significance_probability <- function(df_outcome,
                                          pred_df,
                                          thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                          jitter_height = 0.04) {
  ggplot(df_outcome, aes(x = range, y = significant_num)) +
    geom_jitter(
      aes(color = significant_f),
      width = 0,
      height = jitter_height,
      alpha = 0.45,
      size = 2
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
      linewidth = 1.2,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = thresholds,
      linetype = "dashed",
      alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(-0.02, 1.02),
      breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
      labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Probability of Significant Cyclicity: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Predicted probability of significant GAM",
      color = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# =========================================================
# 9. OPTIONAL: EMPIRICAL BINNED PROBABILITY PLOT
# =========================================================
# This shows the observed proportion significant within bins of range.
make_empirical_bins <- function(df_outcome, n_bins = 10) {
  df_outcome %>%
    mutate(range_bin = ntile(range, n_bins)) %>%
    group_by(range_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_range = mean(range, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(significant_num, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

plot_empirical_and_model <- function(df_outcome, pred_df, empirical_df,
                                     thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50)) {
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = empirical_df,
      aes(x = mean_range, y = prop_significant, size = n),
      alpha = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      aes(x = range, y = pred_prob, linetype = model),
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = thresholds,
      linetype = "dashed",
      alpha = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(0, 1),
      labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Empirical and Modeled Probability: ", unique(df_outcome$variable)),
      x = "Cyclical range",
      y = "Probability of significant GAM",
      size = "Bin n",
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
}

# =========================================================
# 10. ALL-IN-ONE ANALYSIS FOR ONE VARIABLE
# =========================================================
analyze_one_outcome <- function(df, target_var,
                                thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50),
                                n_bins = 10) {
  df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(df)
  df_outcome <- get_outcome_df(df_clean, target_var)
  
  if (nrow(df_outcome) == 0) {
    stop(paste("No rows found for variable:", target_var))
  }
  
  desc <- summarize_cyclicity(df_outcome)
  thresh_tab <- threshold_table(df_outcome, thresholds = thresholds)
  mods <- fit_logistic_models(df_outcome)
  pred_df <- make_prediction_df(
    df_outcome = df_outcome,
    model_range = mods$model_range,
    model_range_n = mods$model_range_n
  )
  emp_df <- make_empirical_bins(df_outcome, n_bins = n_bins)
  
  p_hist <- plot_range_histogram(df_outcome)
  p_prob <- plot_significance_probability(df_outcome, pred_df, thresholds = thresholds)
  p_emp <- plot_empirical_and_model(df_outcome, pred_df, emp_df, thresholds = thresholds)
  
  list(
    data = df_outcome,
    descriptives_overall = desc$overall,
    descriptives_by_significance = desc$by_significance,
    threshold_table = thresh_tab,
    model_range = mods$model_range,
    model_range_n = mods$model_range_n,
    model_range_tidy_OR = mods$tidy_range,
    model_range_n_tidy_OR = mods$tidy_range_n,
    predictions = pred_df,
    empirical_bins = emp_df,
    plot_histogram = p_hist,
    plot_probability = p_prob,
    plot_empirical = p_emp
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 11. RUN IT FOR DRSP_NA
# =========================================================
# Replace gam_df with your actual dataset object name
# result_drsp_na <- analyze_one_outcome(gam_df, target_var = "drsp_NA")

# Then inspect:
# result_drsp_na$descriptives_overall
# result_drsp_na$descriptives_by_significance
# result_drsp_na$threshold_table
# result_drsp_na$model_range_tidy_OR
# result_drsp_na$model_range_n_tidy_OR
# result_drsp_na$plot_histogram
# result_drsp_na$plot_probability
# result_drsp_na$plot_empirical

# =========================================================
# 12. SAVE PLOTS IF YOU WANT
# =========================================================
# ggsave("drsp_NA_histogram_by_significance.png", result_drsp_na$plot_histogram, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("drsp_NA_probability_of_significance.png", result_drsp_na$plot_probability, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("drsp_NA_empirical_probability.png", result_drsp_na$plot_empirical, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# =========================================================
# 13. RUN ACROSS ALL DRSP VARIABLES
# =========================================================
get_drsp_variables <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    clean_gam_summary() %>%
    distinct(variable) %>%
    pull(variable) %>%
    .[str_detect(., regex("^drsp", ignore_case = TRUE))]
}

summarize_all_drsp_vars <- function(df,
                                    thresholds = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50)) {
  df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(df)
  drsp_vars <- get_drsp_variables(df_clean)
  
  map_dfr(drsp_vars, function(v) {
    df_v <- get_outcome_df(df_clean, v)
    
    if (nrow(df_v) < 5) return(NULL)
    
    mod1 <- try(glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_v), silent = TRUE)
    mod2 <- try(glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_v), silent = TRUE)
    
    th_tab <- threshold_table(df_v, thresholds = thresholds) %>%
      pivot_wider(
        names_from = threshold,
        values_from = c(n_at_or_above, prop_of_sample, n_significant, prop_significant),
        names_sep = "_th_"
      )
    
    out <- tibble(
      variable = v,
      n_participants = nrow(df_v),
      n_significant = sum(df_v$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_significant = mean(df_v$significant_num == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_range = median(df_v$range, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25_range = quantile(df_v$range, .25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75_range = quantile(df_v$range, .75, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_n = mean(df_v$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
    
    if (!inherits(mod1, "try-error")) {
      tidy1 <- broom::tidy(mod1, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
        filter(term == "range") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_range = estimate,
          OR_range_low = conf.low,
          OR_range_high = conf.high,
          p_range = p.value
        )
      out <- bind_cols(out, tidy1)
    }
    
    if (!inherits(mod2, "try-error")) {
      tidy2 <- broom::tidy(mod2, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)
      
      range_row <- tidy2 %>%
        filter(term == "range") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_range_adj_n = estimate,
          OR_range_adj_n_low = conf.low,
          OR_range_adj_n_high = conf.high,
          p_range_adj_n = p.value
        )
      
      n_row <- tidy2 %>%
        filter(term == "n") %>%
        transmute(
          OR_n = estimate,
          OR_n_low = conf.low,
          OR_n_high = conf.high,
          p_n = p.value
        )
      
      out <- bind_cols(out, range_row, n_row)
    }
    
    bind_cols(out, th_tab)
  }) %>%
    arrange(desc(prop_significant), desc(median_range))
}

# Example:
# all_drsp_summary <- summarize_all_drsp_vars(gam_df)
# View(all_drsp_summary)

# =========================================================
# 14. OPTIONAL: FIND THE 50% CROSSING FROM THE SIMPLE MODEL
# =========================================================
# This is not a "natural breakpoint"; it is just the range value
# where predicted probability of significance = 0.50.
get_p50_threshold <- function(model_range) {
  coefs <- coef(model_range)
  if (length(coefs) < 2 || is.na(coefs[1]) || is.na(coefs[2]) || coefs[2] == 0) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -coefs[1] / coefs[2]
}

# Example:
# p50_drsp_na <- get_p50_threshold(result_drsp_na$model_range)
# p50_drsp_na

# =========================================================
# 15. VERY SHORT MINIMAL VERSION FOR JUST drsp_NA
# =========================================================
# Uncomment and run this if you want the leanest version only.
#
# df_clean <- clean_gam_summary(gam_df)
# df_na <- df_clean %>% filter(variable == "drsp_NA")
#
# m1 <- glm(significant_num ~ range, family = binomial, data = df_na)
# m2 <- glm(significant_num ~ range + n, family = binomial, data = df_na)
#
# pred_df <- make_prediction_df(df_na, m1, m2)
#
# ggplot(df_na, aes(range, significant_num)) +
#   geom_jitter(aes(color = significant_f), height = 0.04, width = 0, alpha = 0.45) +
#   geom_line(data = pred_df, aes(y = pred_prob, linetype = model), linewidth = 1.2) +
#   geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.25, 0.36, 0.50), linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.6) +
#   scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Probability of Significant Cyclicity: drsp_NA",
#     x = "Cyclical range",
#     y = "Probability of significant GAM",
#     color = NULL,
#     linetype = NULL
#   )


```

```{r}


result_drsp_na <- analyze_one_outcome(gam_df, target_var = "drsp_NA")

result_drsp_na$descriptives_overall
result_drsp_na$descriptives_by_significance
result_drsp_na$threshold_table
result_drsp_na$model_range_tidy_OR
result_drsp_na$model_range_n_tidy_OR
result_drsp_na$plot_probability
```
